summary:
The患儿’s post-infectious presentation with bloody diarrhea followed by pallor, lethargy, and schistocyte formation supports hemolytic uremic syndrome due to Shiga toxin–producing Escherichia coli after an acute gastrointestinal illness—key differences from TTP include the typical GI prodrome and younger age at onset. This pattern matches classic pediatric HUS pathophysiology better than TTP, especially without significant central nervous system manifestations or other systemic signs suggestive of microangiopathic processes unrelated to gut-derived toxins.  

\boxed{C}

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
